docs.title: NetBox Diode Device Wrapper - Documentation
docs.text: A high-level Python wrapper package for the NetBox Diode SDK that provides a "Device-Centric" simplified interface for managing network infrastructure data in NetBox.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Installation and quickstart tutorial
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation for all classes and methods
  • CLI Reference - Command-line interface documentation
  • Migration Guide - Migrating between versions
  • Architecture - Package structure and design decisions

docs.title: About
docs.text: This package enables network automation engineers to define network devices as Python dictionaries and push them to NetBox Diode with minimal code, while enforcing data integrity and validation.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Pydantic Models: Typed device models with comprehensive validation
  • Converter Layer: Seamless Pydantic-to-Protobuf conversion for Diode
  • CLI Tool: Command-line interface for import/export operations
  • Batch Processing: Efficient handling of large device datasets
  • Exception Hierarchy: Comprehensive error reporting

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install netbox-dio

See the Getting Started guide for detailed installation instructions.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, convert_device

# Create a device from a dictionary
device = DiodeDevice.from_dict({
    "name": "router-01",
    "site": "site-a",
    "device_type": "cisco-9300",
    "role": "core-router"
})

# Convert to protobuf for Diode transmission
entity = convert_device(device)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: This document describes the comprehensive error handling system implemented in the netbox-dio package.

docs.title: Exception Hierarchy
docs.text:
The package implements a complete exception hierarchy rooted at DiodeError, with specialized exception types for different error scenarios.

Exception
└── DiodeError (base class)
    ├── DiodeValidationError (data validation errors)
    │   └── DiodeConversionError (conversion errors with source context)
    ├── DiodeClientError (I/O layer errors)
    │   ├── DiodeConnectionRefusedError (connection refused)
    │   ├── DiodeTimeoutError (connection timeout)
    │   └── DiodeAuthenticationError (auth failures)
    ├── DiodeServerResponseError (server errors)
    └── DiodeBatchError (batch operation errors)

docs.title: DiodeError (Base Exception)
docs.text:
The base class for all Diode-related errors. Provides structured context for debugging.

Features: - Message string for error description - Context dictionary for additional debugging information - Automatic traceback preservation through exception chaining

Usage: 

from netbox_dio import DiodeError

error = DiodeError(
    "Connection failed",
    context={"endpoint": "diode.example.com:443", "retry": 3}
)
print(str(error))  # "Connection failed"
print(error.context)  # {"endpoint": "diode.example.com:443", "retry": 3}

docs.title: DiodeValidationError
docs.text:
Raised when data validation fails (Pydantic ValidationError wrapping).

Context fields: - field_name: The field that failed validation - value: The invalid value provided - device_name: Optional device name for context

Example: 

from netbox_dio import DiodeValidationError
from netbox_dio.models import DiodeDevice

try:
    DiodeDevice.from_dict({"name": "test-device"})  # Missing required fields
except DiodeValidationError as e:
    print(e.context["field_name"])  # "site" (first missing field)
    print(e.context["device_name"])  # None or provided device name

docs.title: DiodeConversionError
docs.text:
Raised when device-to-Diode-entity conversion fails.

Context fields: - device_name: Name of the device being converted - original_dict: The original dictionary being converted - conversion_type: Type of conversion (device, interface, vlan, etc.)

Example: 

from netbox_dio import DiodeConversionError
from netbox_dio.converter import convert_device

try:
    convert_device(device)
except DiodeConversionError as e:
    print(e.context["device_name"])  # "router-01"
    print(e.context["conversion_type"])  # "device"

docs.title: DiodeClientError (I/O Layer Errors)
docs.text: Base class for client-side I/O errors with specific subclasses:

docs.title: DiodeConnectionRefusedError
docs.text:
Raised when the Diode server actively refuses the connection.

Context fields: - endpoint: The endpoint that was refused - original_error: The underlying connection error

docs.title: DiodeTimeoutError
docs.text:
Raised when the connection to Diode times out.

Context fields: - endpoint: The endpoint that timed out - timeout_ms: Optional timeout duration

docs.title: DiodeAuthenticationError
docs.text:
Raised when authentication fails (401/403 responses).

Context fields: - endpoint: The Diode endpoint - original_error: The underlying auth error

Example: 

from netbox_dio import DiodeAuthenticationError
from netbox_dio import DiodeClient

client = DiodeClient.from_env()

try:
    client.connect()
except DiodeAuthenticationError as e:
    print(f"Auth failed for {e.context['endpoint']}")
    print(e)  # "Authentication failed for diode.example.com:443: ..."

docs.title: DiodeServerResponseError
docs.text:
Raised when the Diode server returns an error response (4xx/5xx).

Context fields: - request_id: Server's request ID for debugging - device_name: Name of the device that caused the error - status_code: HTTP status code from server

docs.title: DiodeBatchError
docs.text:
Raised when batch operations fail.

Context fields: - errors: List of individual error objects - total: Total items in batch - failed: Number of failed items - summary: Dict mapping error types to counts

docs.title: Pattern 1: Try-Except with Specific Exceptions
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeClient, DiodeAuthenticationError, DiodeTimeoutError

client = DiodeClient.from_env()

try:
    client.connect()
    client.send_single(device)
except DiodeAuthenticationError as e:
    print(f"Authentication failed: {e}")
    # Handle auth failure - prompt for new credentials
except DiodeTimeoutError as e:
    print(f"Connection timeout: {e}")
    # Handle timeout - retry with backoff
except DiodeClientError as e:
    print(f"Client error: {e}")
    # Handle other client errors

docs.title: Pattern 2: Exception Context for Debugging
docs.text:
try:
    device = DiodeDevice.from_dict(data)
except DiodeValidationError as e:
    # Access context for debugging
    print(f"Field: {e.context.get('field_name')}")
    print(f"Value: {e.context.get('value')}")
    print(f"Device: {e.context.get('device_name')}")
    # Log full error with context
    logger.error("Validation failed", extra=e.context)

docs.title: Pattern 3: Batch Error Aggregation
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import BatchProcessor, BatchResult

processor = BatchProcessor(max_chunk_size=100)
result = processor.process_batch(client, devices)

if result.has_errors():
    print(f"Batch completed: {result.success}/{result.total} successful")

    # Get error summary
    for error_type, count in result.get_error_summary().items():
        print(f"  {error_type}: {count} errors")

    # Get failed device names
    for device_name in result.get_failed_devices():
        print(f"  Failed: {device_name}")

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always catch specific exceptions first: Catch DiodeAuthenticationError before DiodeClientError to handle auth failures differently.

  2. Use exception context for debugging: The context dictionary contains valuable debugging information including field names, values, and device names.

  3. Log full exception chains: Use logger.exception() to capture full traceback and context.

  4. Aggregate batch errors: For batch operations, use BatchResult.get_error_summary() to get a breakdown of error types.

  5. Preserve error context: When re-raising exceptions, use the original_error parameter to preserve the original cause.

docs.title: Error Messages
docs.text:
All exception classes provide informative error messages:

# ValidationError with context
DiodeValidationError(
    "Device 'router-01': status 'invalid_status' is not valid. Must be one of: active, offline, planned",
    field_name="status",
    value="invalid_status",
    device_name="router-01"
)

# Conversion error with context
DiodeConversionError(
    "Conversion error for device 'router-01': SDK error",
    device_name="router-01",
    conversion_type="device"
)

# I/O error with endpoint
DiodeAuthenticationError(
    "Authentication failed for device 'router-01': 401 Unauthorized",
    endpoint="diode.example.com:443"
)

docs.title: From Generic Exception Handling
docs.text:
Before: 

try:
    client.connect()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")

After: 

try:
    client.connect()
except DiodeAuthenticationError as e:
    print(f"Auth failed for {e.context['endpoint']}")
except DiodeTimeoutError as e:
    print(f"Timeout connecting to {e.context['endpoint']}")
except DiodeClientError as e:
    print(f"Client error: {e}")

docs.title: Exception Classes
docs.text: Class Module Description DiodeError netbox_dio Base exception class DiodeValidationError netbox_dio Validation errors DiodeConversionError netbox_dio Conversion errors DiodeClientError netbox_dio I/O layer errors DiodeConnectionRefusedError netbox_dio Connection refused DiodeTimeoutError netbox_dio Connection timeout DiodeAuthenticationError netbox_dio Auth failures DiodeServerResponseError netbox_dio Server errors DiodeBatchError netbox_dio Batch operation errors

docs.title: DiodeDevice
docs.text:
The primary device model representing a network device in NetBox.

from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice

# Create from dictionary
device = DiodeDevice.from_dict({
    "name": "router-01",
    "site": "site-a",
    "device_type": "cisco-9300",
    "role": "core-router",
    "status": "active",
    "serial": "SN123456",
    "asset_tag": "AT-001",
    "comments": "Core router for campus network",
    "tags": ["network", "core"],
    "custom_fields": {
        "environment": "production",
        "owner": "network-team"
    }
})

# Access properties
print(device.name)
print(device.site)
print(device.device_type)

Attributes: - name (str): Device name - site (str): Site name - device_type (str): Device type model - role (str): Device role - status (str): Device status - serial (str): Serial number - asset_tag (str): Asset tag - comments (str): Device comments - tags (List[str]): Device tags - custom_fields (Dict[str, Any]): Custom fields

docs.title: DiodeInterface
docs.text:
Represents a network interface on a device.

from netbox_dio import DiodeInterface, InterfaceType, InterfaceMode

# Create a 10G interface
interface = DiodeInterface(
    name="GigabitEthernet1/0/1",
    type=InterfaceType.SFP,
    enabled=True,
    mgmt_only=False,
    mode=InterfaceMode.TRUNK,
    mtu=1500,
    description="Upstream connection",
    vlans=[100, 200]
)

# Create from dictionary
interface_dict = {
    "name": "GigabitEthernet1/0/1",
    "type": "sfp",
    "enabled": True,
    "description": "Upstream connection"
}
interface = DiodeInterface.from_dict(interface_dict)

Attributes: - name (str): Interface name - type (InterfaceType): Interface type - enabled (bool): Is interface enabled - mgmt_only (bool): Management-only interface - mode (InterfaceMode): Switchport mode - mtu (int): Maximum transmission unit - description (str): Interface description - vlans (List[int]): Associated VLAN IDs - untagged_vlan (int): Untagged VLAN

docs.title: DiodeVLAN
docs.text:
Represents a VLAN configuration.

from netbox_dio import DiodeVLAN, VLANStatus, VLANRole

vlan = DiodeVLAN(
    name="Management",
    vid=100,
    site="site-a",
    status=VLANStatus.ACTIVE,
    role=VLANRole.CORE,
    description="Management VLAN"
)

docs.title: DiodeModule
docs.text:
Represents a module (line card, module bay) in a device.

from netbox_dio import DiodeModule, ModuleStatus

module = DiodeModule(
    name="Module 1",
    position="1",
    status=ModuleStatus.POWERED,
    description="100G line card"
)

docs.title: DiodeCable
docs.text:
Represents a physical cable connection.

from netbox_dio import DiodeCable, CableType, CableStatus

cable = DiodeCable(
    type=CableType.FIBER,
    status=CableStatus.CONNECTED,
    source_port="GigabitEthernet1/0/1",
    dest_port="GigabitEthernet1/0/2",
    length=50,
    length_unit="m"
)

docs.title: DiodePrefix
docs.text:
Represents an IP prefix.

from netbox_dio import DiodePrefix, PrefixStatus, PrefixRole

prefix = DiodePrefix(
    prefix="10.0.0.0/24",
    site="site-a",
    status=PrefixStatus.ACTIVE,
    role=PrefixRole.CORE,
    description="Core network subnet"
)

docs.title: DiodeIPAddress
docs.text:
Represents an IP address.

from netbox_dio import DiodeIPAddress, IPAddressStatus, IPAddressRole

ip = DiodeIPAddress(
    address="10.0.0.1/24",
    status=IPAddressStatus.ACTIVE,
    role=IPAddressRole.VIRTUAL,
    description="Gateway address"
)

docs.title: DiodeRack
docs.text:
Represents a rack in a data center.

from netbox_dio import DiodeRack

rack = DiodeRack(
    name="Rack-A1",
    site="site-a",
    type="42U",
    status="active",
    width=19,
    u_height=42,
    weight=100,
    max_weight=500
)

docs.title: DiodePDU
docs.text:
Represents a Power Distribution Unit.

from netbox_dio import DiodePDU

pdu = DiodePDU(
    name="PDU-1",
    site="site-a",
    type="HP-Rack-PDU",
    status="active",
    configuration="3-phase",
    outlets=48
)

docs.title: DiodeCircuit
docs.text:
Represents a power circuit.

from netbox_dio import DiodeCircuit

circuit = DiodeCircuit(
    name="Circuit-1",
    site="site-a",
    status="active",
    provider="Utility Company",
    circuit_id="CIR-12345",
    type="primary",
    capacity=200
)

docs.title: DiodePowerFeed
docs.text:
Represents a power feed from PDU to rack.

from netbox_dio import DiodePowerFeed

power_feed = DiodePowerFeed(
    name="Power-A",
    site="site-a",
    status="active",
    pdu="PDU-1",
    rack="Rack-A1",
    type="single-phase",
    voltage=120,
    amperage=20
)

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text: All exceptions inherit from DiodeError.

docs.title: DiodeError
docs.text:
Base exception for all Diode-related errors.

try:
    device = DiodeDevice.from_dict(invalid_data)
except DiodeError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")

docs.title: DiodeValidationError
docs.text: Raised when device data fails validation.

docs.title: DiodeConversionError

docs.title: DiodeClientError

docs.title: DiodeBatchError

docs.title: DiodeConnectionRefusedError
docs.text: Raised when connection to Diode server is refused.

docs.title: DiodeTimeoutError
docs.text: Raised when connection to Diode server times out.

docs.title: DiodeAuthenticationError
docs.text: Raised when authentication fails.

docs.title: convert_device
docs.text:
Convert a DiodeDevice to Protobuf entities.

from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, convert_device

device = DiodeDevice.from_dict({...})
entities = convert_device(device)

docs.title: convert_device_to_entities
docs.text:
Convert a DiodeDevice with all subcomponents.

from netbox_dio import convert_device_to_entities

entities = convert_device_to_entities(device)

docs.title: convert_interface
docs.text:
Convert a DiodeInterface to Protobuf.

from netbox_dio import convert_interface

interface = DiodeInterface.from_dict({...})
proto_interface = convert_interface(interface)

docs.title: convert_device_with_subcomponents
docs.text:
Convert a device with all its subcomponents.

from netbox_dio import convert_device_with_subcomponents

entities = convert_device_with_subcomponents(device)

docs.title: DiodeClient
docs.text:
Client for connecting to NetBox Diode.

from netbox_dio import DiodeClient, ConnectionConfig

client = DiodeClient(
    config=ConnectionConfig(
        endpoint="https://diode.example.com:443",
        client_id="your-client-id",
        client_secret="your-client-secret"
    )
)

# Import devices
client.import_devices([device1, device2])

Methods: - import_devices(devices): Import devices to Diode - export_devices(query): Export devices from Diode - validate_devices(devices): Validate devices without importing

docs.title: BatchProcessor
docs.text:
Process devices in batches.

from netbox_dio import BatchProcessor

processor = BatchProcessor(
    client=client,
    chunk_size=100
)

processor.process(devices)

docs.title: InterfaceType
docs.text:
• SFP: SFP interface
  • SFP_PLUS: SFP+
  • QSFP: QSFP
  • QSFP_PLUS: QSFP+
  • RJ45: RJ45 Ethernet
  • FIBER: Fiber optic

docs.title: InterfaceMode
docs.text:
• ACCESS: Access mode
  • TRUNK: Trunk mode
  • HYBRID: Hybrid mode

docs.title: VLANStatus
docs.text:
• ACTIVE: Active VLAN
  • RESERVED: Reserved
  • DAVISHED: DAVISHED

docs.title: CableType
docs.text:
• FIBER: Fiber optic
  • COAXIAL: Coaxial
  • A_BALANCED: A balanced
  • B_BALANCED: B balanced
  • OTHER: Other

docs.title: PrefixStatus
docs.text:
• ACTIVE: Active
  • RESERVED: Reserved
  • DAVISHED: DAVISHED

docs.title: IPAddressStatus
docs.text:
• ACTIVE: Active
  • RESERVED: Reserved
  • DAVISHED: DAVISHED

docs.title: Create a device with interfaces
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, DiodeInterface

device = DiodeDevice.from_dict({
    "name": "switch-01",
    "site": "dc1",
    "device_type": "cisco-9300",
    "role": "access-switch",
    "interfaces": [
        {
            "name": "GigabitEthernet1/0/1",
            "type": "sfp",
            "enabled": True
        },
        {
            "name": "GigabitEthernet1/0/2",
            "type": "sfp",
            "enabled": False
        }
    ]
})

docs.title: Export to JSON
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import to_json, export_devices

json_output = to_json([device1, device2])
# or
json_output = export_devices([device1, device2], format="json")

docs.title: Import from YAML
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import from_file, validate_import

devices = from_file("devices.yaml")
validation = validate_import(devices)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Understanding the package architecture helps with customization and contribution.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Overview
  • Core Components
  • Models Layer
  • Converter Layer
  • I/O Layer
  • CLI Layer
  • Batch Processing
  • Exception Hierarchy
  • Data Flow

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The package follows a layered architecture pattern:

+------------------+
|     CLI Layer    |
+------------------+
|     I/O Layer    |
+------------------+
|  Converter Layer |
+------------------+
|   Models Layer   |
+------------------+

docs.title: Design Goals
docs.text:
• Device-Centric: Define devices as Python dictionaries
  • Type-Safe: Pydantic models with validation
  • Extensible: Easy to add new model types
  • Efficient: Batch processing for large datasets

docs.title: Models Layer
docs.text:
Location: src/netbox_dio/models/

The models layer defines Pydantic models representing NetBox entities.

Models: - DiodeDevice - Primary device model - DiodeInterface - Network interface - DiodeVLAN - VLAN configuration - DiodeModule - Device module - DiodeCable - Physical cable - DiodePrefix - IP prefix - DiodeIPAddress - IP address - DiodeRack - Data center rack - DiodePDU - Power distribution unit - DiodeCircuit - Power circuit - DiodePowerFeed - Power feed

Key Features: - Pydantic v2 models with validation - from_dict() class method for dictionary conversion - Type hints for all fields - Custom validators

docs.title: Converter Layer
docs.text:
Location: src/netbox_dio/converter.py

The converter layer transforms Pydantic models to Protobuf entities for Diode transmission.

Functions: - convert_device() - Convert a single device - convert_device_to_entities() - Convert device with subcomponents - convert_interface() - Convert interface - convert_vlan() - Convert VLAN - convert_module() - Convert module - convert_module_bay() - Convert module bay - convert_cable() - Convert cable - convert_prefix() - Convert prefix - convert_ip_address() - Convert IP address - convert_device_with_subcomponents() - Convert with all subcomponents

Key Features: - One-to-one mapping to Diode Protobuf entities - Recursive conversion of nested structures - Error handling and validation

docs.title: I/O Layer
docs.text:
Location: src/netbox_dio/io.py

The I/O layer handles communication with the Diode gRPC API.

Classes: - DiodeClient - Main client for Diode operations - ConnectionConfig - Connection configuration - BatchProcessor - Batch processing engine

Methods: - import_devices() - Import devices to Diode - export_devices() - Export devices from Diode - validate_devices() - Validate without importing - process_batches() - Process devices in batches

Key Features: - gRPC client for Diode API - Batch processing support - Error handling and retries - TLS/SSL support

docs.title: CLI Layer
docs.text:
Location: src/netbox_dio/cli/

The CLI layer provides command-line interface for all operations.

Commands: - import - Import devices from files - export - Export devices to files - dry-run - Validate without executing - batch - Process in batches

Key Features: - Typer framework for CLI - Command subcommands for each operation - Configuration file support - Tab completion support

docs.title: Batch Processing
docs.text: The package supports efficient batch processing of large datasets.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Devices are grouped into chunks
  2. Each chunk is processed independently
  3. Errors are handled per-chunk
  4. Progress is tracked

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import BatchProcessor

processor = BatchProcessor(
    client=client,
    chunk_size=1000  # Devices per chunk
)

processor.process(devices)

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Memory efficient: Process chunks instead of all at once
  • Error isolation: One chunk failure doesn't stop all
  • Progress tracking: Track progress chunk by chunk
  • Retry support: Failed chunks can be retried

docs.title: Exception Hierarchy
docs.text:
All exceptions inherit from DiodeError.

DiodeError (base)
├── DiodeValidationError
├── DiodeConversionError
├── DiodeClientError
├── DiodeServerResponseError
├── DiodeBatchError
├── DiodeConnectionRefusedError
├── DiodeTimeoutError
└── DiodeAuthenticationError

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeError, DiodeValidationError

try:
    device = DiodeDevice.from_dict(invalid_data)
except DiodeValidationError as e:
    # Handle validation errors
    print(f"Validation errors: {e.errors}")
except DiodeError as e:
    # Handle other Diode errors
    print(f"Error: {e}")

docs.title: Device Creation Flow
docs.text:
1. User creates dictionary
   ↓
2. DiodeDevice.from_dict()
   ↓
3. Pydantic validation
   ↓
4. Device model object
   ↓
5. convert_device()
   ↓
6. Protobuf entities
   ↓
7. DiodeClient.import_devices()
   ↓
8. gRPC transmission

docs.title: Export Flow
docs.text:
1. DiodeClient.export_devices()
   ↓
2. Protobuf entities from Diode
   ↓
3. Converter functions
   ↓
4. Pydantic model objects
   ↓
5. Export to JSON/YAML/NetBox YAML

docs.title: Adding New Models
docs.text:
1. Create model class in src/netbox_dio/models/
  2. Add to __init__.py exports
  3. Create converter function in converter.py
  4. Add to __init__.py exports

docs.title: Adding New CLI Commands
docs.text:
1. Create command file in src/netbox_dio/cli/commands/
  2. Register in app.py create_app()
  3. Add to CLI exports

docs.title: Testing Strategy
docs.text:
• Unit tests: Individual components
  • Integration tests: End-to-end flows
  • Mock tests: gRPC interactions
  • Validation tests: Input validation

docs.title: Performance Considerations
docs.text:
• Batch processing for large datasets
  • gRPC for efficient transmission
  • Connection pooling for repeated operations
  • Lazy loading for subcomponents

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for the netbox-dio command-line interface.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Installation
  • Configuration
  • Commands
  • import
  • export
  • dry-run
  • batch
  • Exit Codes

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The CLI is included with the netbox-dio package:

pip install netbox-dio

Verify installation:

netbox-dio --version
netbox-dio --help

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Required DIODE_ENDPOINT Diode gRPC endpoint URL Yes DIODE_CLIENT_ID OAuth2 client ID No DIODE_CLIENT_SECRET OAuth2 client secret No DIODE_CERT_FILE Path to TLS certificate No DIODE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY Skip TLS verification (true/false) No DIODE_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT_DIR Output directory for dry-run mode No

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Create a YAML or JSON configuration file (e.g., .netbox-dio.yaml):

endpoint: "https://diode.example.com:443"
client_id: "${DIODE_CLIENT_ID}"
client_secret: "${DIODE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
dry_run: false
batch:
  enabled: true
  size: 1000
output:
  format: "json"
  pretty: true
logging:
  level: "info"

Or use JSON:

{
  "endpoint": "https://diode.example.com:443",
  "client_id": "${DIODE_CLIENT_ID}",
  "client_secret": "${DIODE_CLIENT_SECRET}",
  "dry_run": false,
  "batch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "size": 1000
  }
}

Use the config file:

netbox-dio import --config .netbox-dio.yaml --file devices.json

docs.title: Configuration Precedence
docs.text:
1. Command-line options (highest priority)
  2. Configuration file
  3. Environment variables
  4. Default values (lowest priority)

docs.title: import
docs.text:
Import devices from JSON or YAML files.

Usage: 

netbox-dio import [OPTIONS]

Options:

Option Description --file, -f Input file path (JSON or YAML) --format, -t Input format: auto, json, yaml (default: auto) --output, -o Output file path --dry-run, -d Validate without making API calls --batch, -b Enable batch processing --chunk-size, -c Batch chunk size (default: 1000) --stdin, -s Input from stdin as string --config Configuration file path

Examples:

Import from file: 

netbox-dio import --file devices.json

Import with dry-run: 

netbox-dio import --file devices.json --dry-run

Import with batch processing: 

netbox-dio import --file devices.json --batch --chunk-size 500

Import from stdin: 

cat devices.json | netbox-dio import --format json

Output to file: 

netbox-dio import --file devices.json --output result.json

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export devices to JSON, YAML, or NetBox YAML format.

Usage: 

netbox-dio export [OPTIONS]

Options:

Option Description --format, -t Output format: json, yaml, netbox-yaml (default: json) --output, -o Output file path --pretty, -p Pretty print output --dry-run, -d Dry-run mode (validate only) --batch, -b Batch mode --chunk-size, -c Batch chunk size --stdin, -s Input from stdin as JSON string

Examples:

Export to JSON: 

netbox-dio export --format json --output devices.json

Export to YAML: 

netbox-dio export --format yaml --output devices.yaml

Export to NetBox YAML: 

netbox-dio export --format netbox-yaml --output netbox-devices.yaml

Export from stdin: 

cat devices.json | netbox-dio export --format yaml

docs.title: dry-run
docs.text:
Run import or export in dry-run mode (validate only).

Usage: 

netbox-dio dry-run [OPTIONS]

Arguments:

Argument Description command Command to dry-run: import or export

Examples:

netbox-dio dry-run import --file devices.json
netbox-dio dry-run export --format json

docs.title: batch
docs.text:
Process devices in batches with configurable chunk sizes.

Usage: 

netbox-dio batch [OPTIONS]

Options:

Option Description --file, -f Input file path (required) --format, -t Input format (default: auto) --chunk-size, -c Chunk size for processing (default: 1000) --output, -o Output file path

Examples:

Process in batches: 

netbox-dio batch --file devices.json --chunk-size 100

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Description 0 Success 1 Error (validation, file not found, etc.) 2 Invalid arguments

docs.title: Bash
docs.text:
Add to ~/.bashrc:

eval "$(_NETBOX_DIO_COMPLETE=source_bash netbox-dio)"

docs.title: Zsh
docs.text:
Add to ~/.zshrc:

eval "$(_NETBOX_DIO_COMPLETE=source_zsh netbox-dio)"

docs.title: Fish
docs.text:
Add to ~/.config/fish/config.fish:

eval (env _NETBOX_DIO_COMPLETE=source_fish netbox-dio)

docs.title: Complete Workflow
docs.text:
1. Import devices from an existing system: 

    netbox-dio import --file existing-devices.json --output converted-devices.yaml

  2. Validate without sending: 

    netbox-dio dry-run import --file converted-devices.yaml

  3. Export with batch processing: 

    netbox-dio export --format yaml --batch --chunk-size 500

docs.title: CI/CD Integration
docs.text:
# In your CI/CD pipeline
netbox-dio import --file devices.json --dry-run
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    netbox-dio import --file devices.json --batch
fi

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to the NetBox Diode Device Wrapper! This guide will help you get up and running quickly.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • pip package manager

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text: pip install netbox-dio

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/netboxlabs/diode-sdk-python.git
cd diode-sdk-python
pip install -e .

docs.title: Development installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/netboxlabs/diode-sdk-python.git
cd diode-sdk-python
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: First Device
docs.text: Let's create your first device and send it to NetBox Diode.

docs.title: Step 1: Import the package
docs.text: from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, convert_device, DiodeClient

docs.title: Step 2: Create a device dictionary
docs.text:
device_dict = {
    "name": "router-01",
    "site": "datacenter-a",
    "device_type": "cisco-9300",
    "role": "core-router",
    "status": "active",
    "serial": "SN123456",
    "asset_tag": "AT-001",
    "tags": ["network", "production"],
    "custom_fields": {
        "environment": "production",
        "owner": "network-team"
    }
}

docs.title: Step 3: Create the DiodeDevice
docs.text:
device = DiodeDevice.from_dict(device_dict)
print(f"Created device: {device.name}")

docs.title: Step 4: Convert to Diode format
docs.text:
entities = convert_device(device)
print(f"Converted to {len(entities)} entities")

docs.title: Step 5: Connect to Diode and import
docs.text:
# Configure connection
config = ConnectionConfig(
    endpoint="https://diode.example.com:443",
    client_id="your-client-id",
    client_secret="your-client-secret"
)

# Create client and import
client = DiodeClient(config=config)
client.import_devices([device])

docs.title: Working with Subcomponents
docs.text: Devices often have interfaces, VLANs, and other subcomponents.

docs.title: Add interfaces to a device
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, DiodeInterface

device = DiodeDevice.from_dict({
    "name": "switch-01",
    "site": "dc1",
    "device_type": "cisco-9300",
    "role": "access-switch",
    "interfaces": [
        {
            "name": "GigabitEthernet1/0/1",
            "type": "sfp",
            "enabled": True,
            "description": "Uplink to core"
        },
        {
            "name": "GigabitEthernet1/0/2",
            "type": "sfp",
            "enabled": True,
            "mode": "trunk",
            "vlans": [100, 200, 300]
        }
    ]
})

docs.title: Add VLANs
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import DiodeVLAN, VLANStatus

vlan = DiodeVLAN.from_dict({
    "name": "Management",
    "vid": 100,
    "site": "dc1",
    "status": "active"
})

docs.title: Using the CLI
docs.text: The package includes a command-line interface for importing and exporting devices.

docs.title: Configure Diode endpoint
docs.text:
export DIODE_ENDPOINT="https://diode.example.com:443"
export DIODE_CLIENT_ID="your-client-id"
export DIODE_CLIENT_SECRET="your-client-secret"

docs.title: Import from JSON
docs.text: netbox-dio import --file devices.json

docs.title: Import from YAML
docs.text: netbox-dio import --file devices.yaml --format yaml

docs.title: Export to JSON
docs.text: netbox-dio export --format json --output devices.json

docs.title: Dry-run (validate only)
docs.text: netbox-dio dry-run import --file devices.json

docs.title: Batch processing
docs.text:
For large datasets, use batch processing:

from netbox_dio import BatchProcessor

processor = BatchProcessor(
    client=client,
    chunk_size=100
)

processor.process(devices)

docs.title: Import from NetBox API
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import from_netbox_api

devices = from_netbox_api(
    url="https://netbox.example.com",
    token="your-token",
    filters={"site": "dc1"}
)

docs.title: Export to NetBox YAML
docs.text:
from netbox_dio import to_netbox_yaml

yaml_output = to_netbox_yaml([device1, device2])
print(yaml_output)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• API Reference - Learn about all available classes and methods
  • CLI Reference - Master the command-line interface
  • Migration Guide - Learn about version changes
  • Architecture - Understand the package design

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• GitHub Issues
  • Discord Community

docs.title: Migration Guide
docs.text: This guide helps you migrate between versions of NetBox Diode Device Wrapper.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Migrating from v1.0 to v1.1
  • Migrating from v1.1 to v1.2
  • Breaking Changes
  • Deprecation Notice

docs.title: Migrating from v1.0 to v1.1
docs.text: v1.1 introduces a new CLI tool and enhanced export/import capabilities.

docs.title: CLI Tool
docs.text:
A new command-line interface has been added. If you were using raw Python code:

Before (v1.0): 

from netbox_dio import DiodeClient, convert_device
from netbox_dio.export import to_json

# Convert and export
entities = convert_device(device)
json_data = to_json(entities)

After (v1.1): 

# Use the CLI
netbox-dio export --format json --output devices.json
netbox-dio import --file devices.json

docs.title: New Export Options
docs.text:
v1.1 adds new export formats:

from netbox_dio import to_netbox_yaml

# Export in NetBox's YAML format
yaml_output = to_netbox_yaml([device1, device2])

docs.title: Import from NetBox API
docs.text:
New import capability from existing NetBox instances:

from netbox_dio import from_netbox_api

# Import from existing NetBox
devices = from_netbox_api(
    url="https://netbox.example.com",
    token="your-token"
)

docs.title: Migrating from v1.1 to v1.2
docs.text: v1.2 focuses on documentation, configuration, and enhanced validation.

docs.title: Configuration File Support
docs.text:
v1.2 introduces configuration file support for the CLI:

Create a config file (.netbox-dio.yaml): 

endpoint: "https://diode.example.com:443"
client_id: "${DIODE_CLIENT_ID}"
client_secret: "${DIODE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
dry_run: false
batch:
  size: 1000
  enabled: true

Use the config: 

netbox-dio import --config .netbox-dio.yaml --file devices.json

docs.title: Enhanced Validation
docs.text:
v1.2 adds more comprehensive validation rules:

from netbox_dio import DiodeDevice, DiodeValidationError

try:
    device = DiodeDevice.from_dict(invalid_data)
except DiodeValidationError as e:
    # Enhanced error messages in v1.2
    print(e.errors)

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
v1.2 includes comprehensive documentation:

# Read the documentation
# Online: https://netbox-dio.readthedocs.io/
# Local: docs/ directory

docs.title: API Changes
docs.text: No breaking API changes between versions. All existing code should continue to work.

docs.title: CLI Changes
docs.text:
The CLI has been enhanced but maintains backward compatibility with v1.1:

  • All v1.1 commands work in v1.2
  • New --config option available
  • New --batch option available

docs.title: Model Changes
docs.text: No model changes. The DiodeDevice model structure remains the same.

docs.title: Deprecation Notice
docs.text: None currently.

docs.title: Upgrade via pip
docs.text: pip install --upgrade netbox-dio

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text: python -c "import netbox_dio; print(netbox_dio.__version__)"

docs.title: Update your code
docs.text: Most code will work without changes. Check the new features in v1.1 and v1.2 for enhancements you may want to adopt.
